content:
URGENT FIX — QR FLOW OPENS RAW API.QRSERVER IMAGE AND APPEARS TO CRASH

The Android app is leaving IVX and opening Chrome directly on:

api.qrserver.com/v1/...

This is not the expected user flow. The raw QR PNG is being opened as a browser page instead of being rendered inside the app.

Complete this end to end.

1. Find every QR-code implementation in the repository.

Search for:

- api.qrserver.com
- QRCode
- qrImage
- qr_image_url
- qrUrl
- Linking.openURL
- WebView
- window.open
- router.push
- href
- payment QR
- login QR
- share QR

2. Identify the exact route and component that caused the screenshot.

Return:

QR_SOURCE_FILE=
QR_COMPONENT=
QR_TRIGGER_BUTTON=
CURRENT_NAVIGATION_BEHAVIOR=

3. Stop opening the QR image URL externally.

Remove any behavior equivalent to:

Linking.openURL(qrImageUrl)
window.open(qrImageUrl)
router.push(qrImageUrl)

A QR image URL must be rendered as an image, not opened as a webpage.

4. Render the QR code inside the IVX app.

Required UI:

- Modal or dedicated IVX screen
- QR image centered
- Correct title
- Explanation of what the QR code does
- Close button
- Back button
- Share button
- Copy destination link button
- Loading state
- Error state
- Retry button

5. Separate these values correctly:

QR_IMAGE_URL = image displayed inside the app
QR_DESTINATION_URL = link encoded inside the QR
RETURN_URL = route used to return to IVX

Never use QR_IMAGE_URL as the navigation destination.

6. Prefer generating the QR code locally in React Native using the project’s QR component/library rather than depending on a public image endpoint.

The QR code should be generated from QR_DESTINATION_URL inside the app.

Do not expose secrets, tokens, passwords, or private session credentials inside the QR payload.

7. If api.qrserver.com remains as a fallback:

- Fetch it only as an image.
- Validate HTTP 200.
- Validate Content-Type starts with image/.
- Add timeout handling.
- Do not navigate the browser to it.
- Do not allow it to replace the current IVX route.

8. Add safe navigation handling.

Verify:

- Android hardware back returns to the previous IVX screen.
- Closing the modal returns to IVX.
- Browser is not opened automatically.
- Repeated taps do not open multiple screens.
- App state remains intact after background/foreground.
- Invalid QR data does not crash the app.

9. Add diagnostics around the real error.

Capture:

- route
- component
- action
- QR image request status
- destination URL validation
- navigation target
- platform
- trace ID

Do not log private QR payloads.

10. Test on Android:

- Open QR once.
- Close it.
- Reopen it.
- Rotate device.
- Press hardware back.
- Disable internet.
- Restore internet.
- Use invalid QR data.
- Restart app.
- Confirm no Chrome tab opens automatically.
- Confirm no white screen or crash.

11. Test iOS and web parity.

12. Commit, push, deploy, and verify the correct production build/update.

13. Return evidence only:

QR_SOURCE_FILE=
ROOT_CAUSE=
QR_IMAGE_RENDERED_IN_APP=YES/NO
EXTERNAL_BROWSER_AUTO_OPEN=YES/NO
ANDROID_BACK_QA=PASS/FAIL
OFFLINE_QA=PASS/FAIL
INVALID_QR_QA=PASS/FAIL
ANDROID_QA=PASS/FAIL
IOS_QA=PASS/FAIL
WEB_QA=PASS/FAIL
COMMIT_SHA=
DEPLOYMENT_OR_UPDATE_ID=
PRODUCTION_QA=
FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED
